channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Garth Brooks responds to sexual assault allegations
item.description: Country music superstar Garth Brooks has issued a statement following a lawsuit accusing him of raping a hair and makeup artist in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Brittney Baird, KRON4

item.title: Fox News ‘Antisemitism Exposed’ Newsletter: Jewish students vow to 'walk proudly' on grim anniversary
item.description: Fox News' "Antisemitism Exposed" newsletter brings you stories on the rising anti-Jewish prejudice across the U.S. and the world.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Boris Johnson: Netanyahu may have bugged my bathroom
item.description: Former United Kingdom Prime Minister Boris Johnson alleged in an interview with The Telegraph that his staff found a “listening device” in his personal bathroom after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu had used it in 2017.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KREX 5

item.title: For Pittsburgh Jews, attack anniversary adds to an already grim October
item.description: PITTSBURGH (AP) — Jewish communities everywhere reacted with horror at last year's Oct. 7 attack by Hamas on Israel, but the approaching one-year commemoration of the assault hits home particularly hard in Pittsburgh's Jewish community, which already marks a grim anniversary each October.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: South Carolina killer mom Susan Smith, who drowned sons, up for release as experts sound alarm
item.description: Those who knew Susan Smith, the South Carolina mother who drowned her two sons in a lake in the 1990s, say she should not be released on parole in a month.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Japan's new leader expresses regret for governing party slush fund scandal, vows stronger defense
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — New Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba opened his first policy speech in office Friday by expressing deep regrets over the governing party's slush funds scandal that dogged his predecessor.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Menendez brothers trial juror: 'It was a battle of the sexes'
item.description: "The men did not believe that Jose (Menendez) had been abusing his sons," Thornton said Thursday on "Banfield.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: As search for Helene's victims drags into second week, sheriff says rescuers 'will not rest'
item.description: PENSACOLA, N.C. (AP) — The search for victims of Hurricane Helene dragged into its second week on Friday, as exhausted rescue crews and volunteers continued to work long days — navigating past washed out roads, downed power lines and mudslides — to reach the isolated and the missing.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: For migrant women who land in Colorado looking for jobs, a common answer emerges: No
item.description: As record numbers of South Americans come to the U.S. seeking better economic opportunities, many are landing in communities that are unprepared for them — and sometimes outright hostile.
item.dc:creator: Bianca Vázquez Toness, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Two human cases of bird flu confirmed in California as infection continues to spread in US: CDC
item.description: The Centers of Disease Control confirmed on Thursday that two additional cases of the bird flu were identified in California, upping the number to 16 cases in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WATCH: Washington state woman robbed at gunpoint at ATM amid crime concerns
item.description: Surveillance footage captured a ski-masked suspect stalking a woman and allegedly robbing her while she went to a Seattle, Washington-area ATM.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mixed verdict for 3 Memphis officers convicted in Tyre Nichols' fatal beating
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — Three former Memphis police officers were convicted Thursday in the 2023 fatal beating of Tyre Nichols, but were acquitted of the harshest charges they faced for a death that sparked national protests and calls for broad changes in policing.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, KRISTIN M. HALL and JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Springsteen calls Trump 'most dangerous candidate for president in my lifetime,' backs Harris
item.description: The rock legend said the former president “doesn’t understand the meaning of this country, its history or what it means to be deeply American.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man cashes in $500K lottery ticket, finds another winning ticket for same payout a month later
item.description: A man from Washington, D.C., is another $500,000 richer after cashing in a second winning lottery ticket for a DC Lottery drawing in just as many months.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, WGN 9

item.title: American flag stands strong after tourist town leveled by remnants of Hurricane Helene: 'Hope and strength'
item.description: An American flag was placed on an iconic overlook in a North Carolina state park that was decimated after the powerful remnants of Hurricane Helene wrecked havoc on the town in the Blue Ridge Mountains.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Los Angeles prosecutors to review new evidence in Menendez brothers' 1996 murder conviction
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Prosecutors in Los Angeles are reviewing new evidence in the case of Erik and Lyle Menendez to determine whether they should be serving life sentences for killing their parents in their Beverly Hills mansion more than 35 years ago, the city's district attorney said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER and JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Penn. Republican Senate candidate David McCormick: 'I'm the underdog'
item.description: “I'm the underdog,” David McCormick told NewsNation’s Leland Vittert in a one-on-one interview for “On Balance.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Earth's 2nd moon has arrived: What you need to know
item.description: A second moon has officially entered Earth's orbit—sort of. Although it's being called a "mini-moon," it's actually an asteroid named 2024 PT5. The asteroid has been temporarily captured by Earth's gravitational pull. The asteroid will remain in Earth's orbit from now until Nov.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Lewis, KTLA 5

item.title: Oregon woman gets probation after freezing puppies to feed snake, gets to keep pets
item.description: An Oregon woman was sentenced on Wednesday on several animal abuse charges after authorities learned she froze several litters of puppies to feed her pet snakes.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Bourgeois, Fox 5

item.title: Eight city workers arrested in Columbus Animal Care & Control investigation
item.description: "This investigation also uncovered multiple criminal violations that are part of the Georgia State Code," Deputy Police Chief Lance Deaton said.
item.dc:creator: Hannah James, NewsNation

item.title: Navy veteran student recounts saving his ASU classmate from stabbing attack: 'Compelled to do something'
item.description: An Arizona State University student sprang into action to help classmate during "random" stabbing attack on the campus, saying that he felt "compelled" to help.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DC man cashes in $500K lottery ticket, finds another winning ticket for same payout
item.description: "Phillip was mum about his second winning ticket because he couldn't find it," according to the lottery's website.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, NewsNation

item.title: Dockworkers' union to suspend strike until Jan. 15
item.description: The union representing 45,000 striking U.S. dockworkers at East and Gulf coast ports reached a deal Thursday to suspend a three-day strike until Jan. 15 to provide time to negotiate a new contract.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Gallego rakes in close to $22M in third quarter for Arizona Senate race
item.description: Rep. Ruben Gallego (D-Ariz.) raised close to $22 million in the third quarter of fundraising for this year in the Arizona Senate race, his campaign announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Subway rider shot in the head by police files claim accusing officers of recklessly opening fire
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A lawyer for a New York City subway rider who was inadvertently shot in the head by police at a station has filed a legal claim against the city, accusing the officers of showing “carelessness and reckless” disregard for the lives of others when they opened fire on a man holding
item.dc:creator: CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dockworkers' union to suspend strike until Jan. 15 to allow time to negotiate new contract
item.description: The union representing 45,000 striking U.S. dockworkers at East and Gulf coast ports has reached a deal to suspend a three-day strike until Jan. 15 to provide time to negotiate a new contract.
item.dc:creator: Tom Krisher, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: DC man cashes in $500K lottery ticket, finds another winning ticket for same payout a month later
item.description: A man from Washington, D.C., is another $500,000 richer after cashing in a second winning lottery ticket for a DC Lottery drawing in just as many months.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, Fox 5

item.title: Ex-NYPD boss rejected discipline for cops who raided bar where owner says he was 'extorted'
item.description: In late August, shortly before resigning under a cloud of scandal, New York City’s former police commissioner blocked the discipline of three officers involved in a melee outside a Brooklyn juice bar.
item.dc:creator: Jake Offenhartz, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Twin babies who died alongside their mother are youngest-known Helene victims
item.description: A large tree had crashed through the roof, crushing Kobe Williams and causing her to fall on top of infant sons Khyzier and Khazmir. All three were found dead.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 3 officers convicted in Tyre Nichols fatal beating, 2 acquitted of civil rights charges
item.description: Three former Memphis officers were convicted Thursday of charges of witness tampering in the 2023 fatal beating of Tyre Nichols, and two were acquitted of federal civil rights violations in a death that sparked national protests and calls for broad changes in policing.
item.dc:creator: Megan Fayard, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Los Angeles prosecutors to review new evidence in Menendez brothers' 1996 murder conviction
item.description: Prosecutors in Los Angeles say they are reviewing new evidence in the case of the Menendez brothers to determine whether they should be serving life sentences for killing their parents in their Beverly Hills mansion more than 35 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Former Colorado county clerk Tina Peters sentenced to 9 years for voting data scheme
item.description: A judge ripped into a Colorado county clerk for her crimes and lies before sentencing her Thursday to nine years behind bars for a data-breach scheme spawned from the rampant false claims about voting machine fraud in the 2020 presidential race.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Country music star Garth Brooks accused of sexual assault by makeup artist
item.description: Garth Brooks, the country music superstar who gained fame in the 1990s with several hit songs, has been named in a lawsuit accusing him of raping a hair and makeup artist in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, Fox 5

item.title: Trump calls new January 6th accusations 'pure election interference'
item.description: The former president spoke exclusively to NewsNation about the unsealing of documents related to his election interference case.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Doctor who helped supply Matthew Perry ketamine pleads guilty
item.description: A San Diego doctor charged in connection with Matthew Perry’s fatal overdose pleaded guilty Wednesday to conspiring to distribute the surgical anesthetic ketamine.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Hurricanes like Helene are deadly when they strike and keep killing for years to come
item.description: Hurricanes in the United States end up hundreds of times deadlier than the government calculates, contributing to more American deaths than car accidents or all the nation's wars, a new study said. The average storm hitting the U.S.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Donate today to help Red Cross efforts across 10 states
item.description: Days after Hurricane Helene's deadly force tore a path north from the Gulf Coast, many Americans remained without power, water and other basic necessities.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, Fox 5
